channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: ‘Our fight is to fix the system’: India protesters demand accountability
item.description: India's Cockroach Janta Party led weeks of protests over exam paper leaks.

item.title: Which Middle East nations have nuclear programmes, and how were they built?
item.description: Saudi Arabia aims to join the region's nuclear club after it signs a deal with the US.

item.title: Cricket: No Ashes 2027 Tests to take place in the north of England
item.description: England will not host Australia at Old Trafford or Headingley.

item.title: EU hits Google with new $1bn fine, saying it broke digital antitrust rules
item.description: The fine is part of a major crackdown on Big Tech by Brussels which says it is seeking to ensure fair competition.

item.title: Can Iran withstand new pressure from the US?
item.description: After a truce, the US has returned to war with Iran. Alex Gatopoulos looks at what’s different this time

item.title: Iraq PM heads to Iran: Can Baghdad redefine its relationship with Tehran?
item.description: Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaid is visiting Tehran after a high-profile meeting with US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Several killed as Ukraine, Russia trade attacks deep behind the front line
item.description: Four have been killed by Russian strikes in Ukraine, while four others died in Russia and Crimea, according to reports.

item.title: Israel releases 35 detainees from Gaza
item.description: Israel releases 35 detainees from Gaza

item.title: Why a North Sea oilfield is emerging as British PM Burnham’s big test
item.description: Reports suggesting the new British PM might allow drilling in the North Sea have excited Donald Trump, divided locals.

item.title: Women’s Africa Cup of Nations 2026: What to know about the tournament
item.description: Tournament format, venues, title favourites, groups, prize money and players to watch at the 2026 WAFCON in Morocco.

item.title: Rubio says US policy on Iran is ‘a head for an eye’
item.description: Rubio says US policy on Iran is ‘a head for an eye’

item.title: Right-wing Israeli groups storm Al-Aqsa as Palestinians barred from worship
item.description: Israeli police barred Palestinians from entering Al-Aqsa Mosque as more than 2,300 right-wing Israelis entered.

item.title: Norway to make formal complaint to FIFA over Balogun red card saga
item.description: FIFA overturned the suspension for a red card for the USA forward at the World Cup after a request from President Trump.

item.title: Rubio says Iran ‘begging’ for a deal with US but not ready to make one
item.description: Secretary of state says US has 'a head for an eye' policy as strikes hit Iran for a 12th straight night.

item.title: Thai sailors sue owners of cargo ship hit in Hormuz
item.description: Thai sailors sue owners of cargo ship hit in Hormuz

item.title: Iran, Houthis strike tankers as US bombing continues: What’s the latest?
item.description: With the Houthi naval blockade, shipping disruptions now impact the Bab al-Mandeb strait in addition to Hormuz.

item.title: ‘Televising our revolution’: India’s Gen Z flips Modi’s social media game
item.description: For 12 years, Modi and his party have dominated Indian social media. Now, youth use the same platforms against him.

item.title: More than 4,200 Israelis storm Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem
item.description: National Security Minister Ben-Gvir joins settlers and far-right groups at holy site under heavy police protection.

item.title: ‘Back from the dead’: The continuing agony over Gaza’s 9,500 missing people
item.description: Palestinian's sudden return alive after family held his funeral highlights suffering of those still waiting for answers.

item.title: Indian activist Wangchuk sets conditions to end hunger strike
item.description: Indian activist Wangchuk sets conditions to end hunger strike

item.title: Photos: Gaza’s shelter crisis
item.description: Gaza faces a deepening housing crisis as more than 92 percent of homes have been damaged or destroyed by Israel.

item.title: Travellers panic as US attacks Iran-Iraq border crossing
item.description: Travellers panic as US attacks Iran-Iraq border crossing

item.title: India’s Modi promises fast-track courts for exam fraud fuelling protests
item.description: Cockroach Janta Party protesters are occupying streets in New Delhi, demanding Education Minister Pradhan resign.

item.title: Top US and Russian diplomats discuss Ukraine war in Manila
item.description: Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov 'reaffirmed ​readiness' for political ​and diplomatic settlement to Russia-Ukraine war.

item.title: Arsenal defender Saliba sidelined for ‘extended period’ due to back injury
item.description: Centre back William Saliba won't require surgery for injury sustained at World Cup, his club says.
